proven.claim: Prove: after line 26, order.approvedBy is the value of the approver parameter
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 28
; Prove: after line 26, order.approvedBy is the value of the approver parameter
(declare-const approver_id Int)      ; models approver identity (abstracted)
(declare-const approvedBy_stored Int)
; Code transition (line 26): order.approvedBy = approver (direct assignment)
(assert (= approvedBy_stored approver_id))
; Negate: stored value differs from passed approver
(assert (not (= approvedBy_stored approver_id)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: approver is a caller-supplied string parameter with no non-empty validation.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 28
; approver is a caller-supplied string parameter with no non-empty validation.
; TypeScript strict mode prevents null/undefined but permits empty string "".
(declare-const approver_length Int)
(declare-const approvedBy_length Int)
; Code transition (line 26): order.approvedBy = approver (direct, unchecked assignment)
(assert (= approvedBy_length approver_length))
; Calling context: caller passes empty string — TypeScript string type permits this
(assert (= approver_length 0))
; Violation: empty string stored as approver identity and printed in the log
(assert (= approvedBy_length 0))
(check-sat)
